summary: CONSTITUTIONAL CHECKS: ALL 4 PASS — Cantonal satisfied (no new tables, functions operate within existing LCS cantonal structure, errors flow to lcs_errors), Registry-First (N/A — no tables created), Sovereign ID (CID/SID/MID are LCS internal identifiers, not sovereign_company_ids), No sideways calls (FK validations against outreach.outreach and cl.company_identity are legitimate spine operations). ENVELOPE: PASS — all 4 fields match (change_type=feature, architectural_flag=false, doctrine_version=2.8.0). SCOPE: PASS — 2 modified paths within migrations/ and tests/. No protected assets touched. EXECUTION: PASS — all 5 acceptance criteria met structurally. fn_promote_emission implements atomic Lock→validate STAGED→FK validation→INSERT cid_intake→fn_process_cid()→SID+MID→PROMOTED flow with hard-fail RAISE on every error path. fn_promote_emission_batch uses FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED with deterministic ORDER BY created_at and returns promoted count. Schema adaptation from WP's ingest_status/RECEIVED to existing status/STAGED is documented and correct. CRITICAL OBSERVATION (requires builder fix before deployment): fn_promote_emission performs INSERT into lcs_errors and UPDATE to REJECTED/ERROR BEFORE calling RAISE EXCEPTION. The RAISE rolls back ALL changes within the function call (PostgreSQL subtransaction semantics). Error logging and status transitions are therefore LOST at runtime in both single-row and batch paths. Phase 2A's fn_process_emissions handles this correctly by NOT raising after error logging. Builder should restructure to either (a) remove RAISE and return error status, or (b) separate error logging from exception path. ADDITIONAL OBSERVATIONS: (1) Functional overlap with Phase 2A fn_process_emissions — both promote STAGED emissions, SRC3 adds full CID→SID→MID chain. (2) Test count 7-8 vs CS claimed 8 — possible truncation.
